body.title: Granny keeps an MRU of wakeable workers; no-wakeable + none-available -> system_alarm + stalled state + resume
body.intention: I intend that Granny, unable to wake or find any capable worker, PARKS on a system_alarm with a resume control instead of spinning forever.
body.description:
**Blocked on: T-granny-wake-not-launch** (needs the wake-call path first). Completes the Granny narrative from D-shim-frontdoor.

**What to build:**
- Granny keeps an MRU of WAKEABLE workers (availability flag = wakeable, front-door up) and tries them in recency order.
- If NO worker can be woken AND none is available -> raise a system_alarm ('I''M STALLED! HELP!' — shared-attention, reuses the just-shipped alarm primitive), set Granny's state to STALLED, and expose a RESUME control; Granny PARKS (does not cycle forever).
- Resume clears the stalled state and re-attempts dispatch.

**Completion criteria:** with all workers unwakeable/unavailable, a dispatch raises exactly one 'granny-stalled' system_alarm, sets state STALLED, and stops cycling; hitting resume re-attempts. A wakeable worker appearing lets the MRU path proceed without the alarm.

**Test plan (real red->green):** integration — no wakeable workers -> assert one system_alarm (signature 'granny-stalled') + state STALLED + no spin; then mark a worker wakeable + resume -> assert dispatch proceeds. Red before the MRU/stall path; green after.

**Validation (runtime):** a genuinely stalled rack shows Granny STALLED with a resume button and a single 'granny-stalled' alarm (via `uu alarms`) rather than a hot loop. Observable via the alarm + Granny state.

**Logging:** log.error('granny: STALLED — no wakeable/available worker for ticket=%s') at the alarm point; log.info('granny: resumed by operator').

**Observable via:** a 'granny-stalled' system_alarm in ~/.unseen_university/operations/system_alarms/ + Granny state=STALLED.

**Affected files (indicative):** unseen_university/devices/granny/ (MRU + stall + resume), unseen_university/system_alarms.py (raise_alarm caller). Links the shipped T-alarm-callsites + T-vetinari-owns-alarm-escalation (this is a new alarm source that Vetinari will escalate).

**Rollback:** git revert removes the MRU + stall (back to prior dispatch). No migration.

**audit-emphasis:** needs-deep-smell (the anti-infinite-cycle park is the point; a stall that still spins defeats it).

**Scope boundary:** MRU + stall-alarm + park/resume; capability-tag routing is the deferred convergence.



---

**BUILD SPEC (resolved 2026-07-02 — closes the orphaned-front-door detection deferred through the front-door/wake tickets; reuses the shipped raise_alarm primitive).** `raise_alarm` DEDUPS by signature (same signature increments a `count` in ONE file — verified), so the anti-spin metric is the raise COUNT / call count, NOT file count: while STALLED, a second run_once must PARK and not re-raise. Granny state lives under `~/.granny/` (its existing home — no new ~/.unseen_university path).

**File 1 (NEW) `unseen_university/devices/granny/stall_state.py`:**
- `_STATE = Path.home()/".granny"/"stall_state.json"`, `_MRU = Path.home()/".granny"/"mru.json"`. All fail-soft (try/except, missing→default).
- `is_stalled() -> bool`: read _STATE; return its `stalled` flag (default False).
- `set_stalled(ticket_id, reason, now=None)`: write `{stalled:true, ticket:ticket_id, reason, since:<iso>}`. Idempotent.
- `resume() -> None`: delete _STATE (clear stalled). `log.info("granny: resumed by operator")`.
- `record_dispatch(worker_id, now=None)`: prepend worker_id to an MRU list in _MRU (dedup: move-to-front; cap length ~16). 
- `mru_order(candidates) -> list`: return `candidates` sorted so most-recently-dispatched come FIRST; candidates never dispatched keep their relative input order at the end. (Read _MRU; stable sort by recency index.)

**File 2 (MODIFY) `unseen_university/devices/granny/daemon.py`:**
- PARK at the TOP of `run_once` (right after computing `granny_mailbox`): `from .stall_state import is_stalled; if is_stalled(): log.info("granny: STALLED — parked; resume to continue"); return`. THIS is the anti-spin (audit-critical): a stalled Granny does not cycle.
- MRU: at the successful-dispatch point (where `dispatched_this_cycle.add(target)` runs), also call `record_dispatch(target)`.
- STALL DETECTION at the END of run_once (after the ticket loop): `from .stall_state import set_stalled; from .availability import is_available` — if `tickets and not dispatched_this_cycle and not any(is_available(w) for w in workers_cfg if isinstance(workers_cfg.get(w), dict))`: raise ONE alarm and park:
  `raise_alarm("granny-stalled", "unseen_university.devices.granny.daemon", "I'M STALLED! HELP! no wakeable/available worker for pending sprint work")` (import raise_alarm from unseen_university.system_alarms); `set_stalled(<first pending ticket id>, "no wakeable/available worker")`; `log.error("granny: STALLED — no wakeable/available worker for ticket=%s", tid)`. (Condition rationale: nothing dispatched this cycle AND no worker available AND work pending = the orphaned-rack stall. akien-routed work adds "akien" to dispatched_this_cycle, so pure-akien work never stalls. A busy-but-available worker keeps is_available True, so 'busy' is not 'stalled'.)

**PROOF 1 (gates close — the audit-critical anti-spin): `tests/granny/test_granny_stall.py::test_stalled_granny_parks_no_respin`.** Setup a config with one worker, patch `_sprint_tickets`→[a builder ticket], `_cleared_gated_tickets`→[], the stale-watchdogs/handshake→no-ops, and `is_available`→False (no wakeable worker). Spy `patch("unseen_university.devices.granny.daemon.raise_alarm")`. Point stall_state at a tmp `~/.granny` (monkeypatch `stall_state._STATE`/`_MRU` to tmp_path files, and call `resume()` in setup for a clean slate). Call `run_once(config, imap=None)` TWICE. Assert `raise_alarm` spy `call_count == 1` (first run stalls+raises+parks; second run sees is_stalled → early-returns → does NOT raise again). **Stub-first:** the stub omits the PARK (the top-of-run_once is_stalled early-return) so the second run re-detects and re-raises → call_count==2 → RED; impl adds the park → 1 → GREEN. This proves the stall does not spin (a stall that still cycles defeats the point).

**PROOF 2 (MRU recency): `tests/granny/test_granny_stall.py::test_mru_orders_most_recent_first`.** `record_dispatch("A")`, `record_dispatch("B")` (monkeypatched _MRU to tmp); `mru_order(["A","B"]) == ["B","A"]` (most-recent first). Stub: `mru_order` returns input unchanged → `["A","B"]` → RED; impl: recency sort → GREEN. (Both proofs share one stub→impl commit pair: the stub omits the park AND leaves mru_order as identity.)

Non-proof tests (same file): `resume()` clears stalled (is_stalled True→False); a wakeable worker (is_available True) → run_once does NOT stall (no alarm). 

**Scope boundary:** MRU + stall-alarm + park/resume only. Capability-TAG routing is the deferred convergence (T-converge-worker-dispatch). The RESUME control is the `resume()` function + state file (operator/web triggers it); wiring a web/MCP button is downstream. Do NOT change the dispatch/rules logic beyond the park guard + stall detection + record_dispatch call.

**Operational:** re-run `tests/test_granny_bus_dispatch.py`, `tests/granny/`, `tests/test_granny_rules_engine.py` — the park guard + stall detection must not regress normal dispatch (a run with an available worker must NOT stall or park). NOTE: pre-existing CC.0-config failures in those files are unrelated (already ticketed).

**Affected files (concrete):** NEW stall_state.py, NEW tests/granny/test_granny_stall.py; MODIFY daemon.py (park guard + stall detection + record_dispatch).

[error] Every ticket closes proven, or declares itself unproven — no discriminator. A ticket that — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
…and 39 more (lower-severity or capped) — query constraints_get for the full set
body.result: Granny stall-alarm + park/resume shipped (closes the orphaned-front-door detection deferred through the front-door/wake chain). run_once early-returns when is_stalled() (anti-spin PARK); on pending work with nothing dispatched AND no worker available/wakeable it raises ONE granny-stalled system alarm (reuses the shipped raise_alarm primitive — Vetinari escalates it), sets STALLED, and parks; resume() clears it. New stall_state.py holds the flat-file state under ~/.granny/. MRU: record_dispatch() tracks worker recency on every dispatch; mru_order() sorts candidates most-recent-first. Proven x2: anti-spin (975089fc — 2nd run_once while stalled does NOT re-raise, red without the park) + mru recency (9ff0a3cc). NAMED GAP (not silent): mru_order is built+proven but NOT yet consumed by dispatch selection (single-builder = no-op today; consuming it touches the live rules/candidate-selection path and is coupled to multi-worker routing) — filed T-granny-consume-mru-in-dispatch (hold, gated on the convergence). Pre-existing granny CC.0-config test fails unrelated + already ticketed.
